name: NI 43-101 Technical Report for the Neptune Project in Nevada, United States dated March 2016.pdf

document.title: NI 43-101 Technical Report for the Neptune Project in Nevada, United States dated March 2016.pdf
document.description: An exploration-stage technical report prepared for Nevada Sunrise Gold Corp. detailing the geological, geophysical, and exploratory drilling results of the Neptune Lithium Brine Project located in Clayton Valley, Nevada with the aim of identifying lithium brine deposits.

deposit_type_candidate.observed_name: Brine deposit derived from dissolution of lithium and other salts from basin sediments
Brine deposit derived from groundwater enrichment by transport of lithium-enriched groundwater to basin traps from devitrified volcanic rocks in the Montezuma Range
Continuing re-enrichment of lithium in basin sediments and groundwater via upwelling of hydrothermal fluids from a lithium-enriched magma beneath Clayton Valley
A combination of all of these deposit models for lithium deposit genesis
